data.title: DELL, HPQ, AAPL: Trump's 25% Auto Tariffs to Hit $140B Worth of PC Imports
data.description:
Shares of computer and laptop makers Dell Technologies (DELL), HP Inc. (HPQ), and Apple (AAPL) fell by 7.7%, 5.5%, and 7.1%, respectively, in after-hours trading yesterday. U.S. President Donald Trump announced reciprocal tariffs on the much-anticipated "Liberation Day," sending global stock markets into a frenzy. Shockingly, the 25% auto tariffs are also expected to hit billions of dollars in imports of personal computers and laptops.

Dell, HPQ, and Apple stand to be impacted the most, as they are among the biggest sellers of PCs and laptops in the U.S. and abroad. In addition to the auto tariffs, Trump announced 34% tariff on Chinese imports and a 25% import duty on imports from Mexico. Notably, most of these companies source computer parts and components from these two countries. In the meantime, the European Union was hit with a 20% across-the-board tariff, while India had a 26% duty levied on its exports to the U.S.

Trump's Tariffs Continue to Cause Havoc

The information became clear when Reuters reviewed the exhaustive list of tariff codes included in the federal register notice on April 2. The 25% auto tariffs included a surprising four-digit tariff code for computers, one of the largest global import categories into the U.S. According to U.S. Census Bureau data, the U.S. imported $138.5 billion worth of PCs, laptops, and related components in 2024. Meanwhile, auto and related imports were valued at nearly $460 billion last year, bringing the total impact to about $600 billion. Remarkably, there is no separate code for automotive computers, which are the essential components of every modern-day car, truck, and electric vehicle (EV).

Effective May 3, these tariffs will impact a comprehensive list of nearly 150 auto parts, which also includes parts like engines and batteries, and everyday products such as tires and brake hoses. According to the update of auto tariff proclamation, tariffs on vehicle imports took effect at midnight on April 3, while duties on the other items will start after a month.

Which Computer Brand Is the Best?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to understand which American computer stock is most favored by analysts. Investors can consider investing in one of these stocks after thorough research.

Currently, only Dell scores Wall Street's "Strong Buy" consensus rating, with DELL stock offering the highest upside potential of 44.1%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'Get Your Popcorn Ready,' Says Investor About CoreWeave Stock
data.description:
Some big AI news popped last week, as the AI cloud infrastructure firm CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) IPO'd – to significant fanfare.

With Nvidia and OpenAI making significant investments in the firm, there was plenty of hype around CoreWeave and its IPO. Some believed that CRWV's performance would reflect the overall state of the AI industry, and were eagerly waiting to see how the market would react.

CoreWeave traded sideways at first, before rocketing up the charts over the last few days. All told, the share price is up over 50% in the company’s first week of trading.

Though the recent news is quite positive, investor Michael Wiggins De Oliveira is not quite ready to jump on board just yet.

"I can't justify investing in a company that's burning through $5 billion in cash per year with no clear profitability plan," explains the 5-star investor.

Though acknowledging CoreWeave's massive revenue growth – 737% year-over-year in FY 2024 – Wiggins De Oliveira notes that this is only one part of the equation. The investor questions the sustainability of these growth rates, while also noting that the company has a cash flow that is "deeply negative."

Another problematic feature the investor cites is the company's heavy reliance on Microsoft, the source for over 60% of its revenues. The hyperscaler has been making heavy AI capex investments, something that the investor points out could very well change going forward. In addition, this creates quite an unequal partnership between the two parties.

"Microsoft isn’t a charity," reminds Wiggins De Oliveira. "This means that all the negotiation power lies at Microsoft’s feet."

The investor does admit that CoreWeave could skyrocket upwards due to investor excitement, while the addition of another major customer could also give CRWV a large boost. All that being said, Wiggins De Oliveira is not willing to take a chance on the newly public company anytime soon.

"This might turn into a meme-stock frenzy, but I'm not betting on hype—I'm betting on financial sustainability, and CoreWeave doesn't have it," adds Wiggins De Oliveria. "I'll be watching from the sidelines with my popcorn, but my money is staying far away."

Wiggins De Oliveira is therefore rating CRWV a Hold (i.e. Neutral). (To watch Wiggins De Oliveira's track record, click here)

To find good ideas for stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanks' Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanks' equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured investor. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.

data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Alphabet's Google Hustles to Rent Nvidia's AI Servers from Recently Listed CoreWeave
data.description:
Tech biggie Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google is set to rent chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) servers from the recently listed CoreWeave (CRWV). The news was first reported by The Information, citing people familiar with the matter. The two companies are in advanced talks about renting Nvidia's servers, which are equipped with its latest artificial intelligence (AI) chips from the Blackwell lineup. Additionally, Google is exploring the possibility of renting space in CoreWeave's data centers to install its own tensor processing unit (TPU) chips.

Google's Hustle Reveals Nvidia's Chip Shortage

Google's hustle to rent NVDA servers highlights the global shortage of AI machines amid their humongous demand. Google's Cloud unit utilizes these advanced GPUs (graphic processing units) to accelerate its cloud computing customers' model training and inferencing processes. Furthermore, the report suggests that Google is in talks with CoreWeave's competitors for renting their spare GPUs. Notably, Google has already placed orders for over $10 billion worth of Blackwell GPUs.

Nvidia is one of the early backers in CoreWeave and shares a special business relationship with the server renter, helping CoreWeave rapidly set up its data center facilities. CoreWeave operates a mega data center in Denton, Texas, which is being utilized by tech giant and OpenAI-backer Microsoft (MSFT). CoreWeave debuted on the Nasdaq stock exchange last week, with an initial listing price of $40 per share. CRWV stock has surged over 53% since its listing, despite having an unfavorable start.

Is Google a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street remains divided on Alphabet's stock trajectory. On TipRanks, GOOGL stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 27 Buys and 10 Hold ratings. Also, the average Alphabet Class A price target of $212.09 implies 35.1% upside potential from current levels. In the past year, GOOGL stock has gained 1.9%.

data.title: Lucid Stock (LCID) Falls Despite 58% Jump in Q1 Deliveries
data.description:
Lucid Group (LCID) stock fell 6.3% in Wednesday's after-hours trading despite the electric vehicle (EV) maker reporting a 58% year-over-year jump in Q1 2025 deliveries to 3,109 vehicles. LCID stock fell as the revenue estimates disclosed by the company in an SEC filing fell short of Wall Street's expectations.  

Also, electric-vehicles.com or EV reported that the company is facing challenges and continued delays with the delivery of its much-anticipated Gravity SUV due to unresolved safety testing issues, mainly related to the third-row seating. Lucid's interim CEO Marc Winterhoff indicated that U.S. deliveries of the Gravity SUV may begin by the end of April. However, sources told EV that the April volumes for the Gravity SUV are expected to be "very small," with higher production not expected until June or July.

Lucid's Q1 Deliveries Surpass Production but Revenue Disappoints

Lucid's Q1 deliveries of 3,109 vehicles surpassed the company's production of 2,212 units in the quarter. The company disclosed that over 600 additional vehicles are in transit to Saudi Arabia for final assembly. Moreover, Q1 2025 deliveries also reflected an increase from the 3,099 vehicles delivered in Q4 2024. In contrast to the 58% year-over-year jump in Lucid’s first-quarter deliveries, EV giant Tesla (TSLA) reported a 13% decline in its Q1 deliveries to 336,681 units and drastically lagged analysts' expectations amid criticism of CEO Elon Musk's political activities.

Despite LCID’s robust deliveries, investors were disappointed as the company disclosed in an SEC filing that it expects Q1 2025 revenue in the range of $232 million to $236 million, which fell short of consensus expectations of $256.3 million. Amid intense competition in the EV space, Lucid has been offering incentives, including cheaper financing, to attract customers away from cheaper hybrid vehicles to its luxury offerings.

Meanwhile, Lucid also announced a $1 billion convertible senior notes offering due 2030 to "persons reasonably believed to be qualified institutional buyers." The company added that it expects to grant the initial buyers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $100 million principal amount of notes.

Is LCID Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

LCID stock gained on Tuesday after Winterhoff told Fox Business that over the past two months, about 50% of the company's new orders have come from former Tesla drivers due to the backlash against Musk.

Given a tough macro backdrop, tariff wars, and growing competition in the EV space, Wall Street is sidelined on Lucid Group stock. The Hold consensus rating for Lucid stock is based on one Buy, six Holds, and three Sell recommendations. The average LCID stock price target of $2.38 indicates that the stock is fully valued at current levels. Lucid stock is down 20.5% so far this year.

data.title: Zuckerberg Pushes Trump to Kill Meta's Antitrust Trial as Deadline Nears
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg is scrambling to stop the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) from taking his company to court. With the antitrust trial set for April 14, Zuckerberg is urging President Donald Trump's administration to intervene and block the case from moving forward, according to a report from The Wall Street Journal.

The FTC wants to unwind Meta's purchases of Instagram and WhatsApp, arguing that the deals crushed competition. But Zuckerberg contends that both of these deals were previously cleared and that the company faces robust competition from other tech giants like TikTok, Alphabet's (GOOGL) YouTube, X, and LinkedIn.

Zuckerberg Pressures White House to Intervene

In a last-minute effort to halt the case, Zuckerberg has reportedly held multiple meetings with White House officials, urging them to step in. Some administration officials have expressed concerns over Meta’s aggressive push for intervention.

Zuckerberg's move comes as Trump recently removed two Democratic FTC commissioners, leaving just two Republicans in charge. That shake-up has raised concerns that the case could be influenced by politics rather than law.

Even with Meta's push to stop the trial, FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson refuses to step aside. He insists the case must move forward to keep Big Tech in check and prevent unfair business tactics.

Meta Battles Global Regulators

Outside the U.S., Meta is also under fire in Europe. The European Union is weighing penalties for violations of the Digital Markets Act (DMA), increasing pressure on Zuckerberg to secure Washington's support. Enacted in 2023, the DMA seeks to limit the dominance of tech giants and make it easier for users to switch between digital services.

Beyond this, Meta remains under constant watch by regulators worldwide. Authorities continue to probe its advertising strategies, data privacy policies, and content management practices. According to TipRanks' Risk Analysis tool, legal and regulatory risks make up 20.8% of Meta's total risks, higher than the industry average of 16.6%.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

 Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on META stock, based on 44 Buys, three Holds and one Sell. The average META price target of $761.49 implies about 30.4% upside from current levels.

data.title: Tesla's (TSLA) Q1 Vehicle Deliveries Leave Investors Devastated
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) reported its Q1 vehicle deliveries on Wednesday, leaving investors and Wall Street analysts devastated. The company's Q1 sales dropped by 13% year-over-year, marking its weakest sales quarter in three years. Meanwhile, Q1 deliveries declined 32% sequentially, highlighting a sharp slowdown. The figures were largely affected by Tesla CEO Elon Musk's political actions and ties to the Trump administration, along with a series of protests and boycotts that hurt the Tesla brand.

Tesla Stock Reacts

Following the Q1 release, Tesla's stock initially fell by up to 4% in early trading but rebounded to close 5.3% higher. The surge was mainly after Politico reported that Musk would be withdrawing from his role as a governing partner in the coming weeks. However, TSLA stock declined over 8% in after-hours trading.

Overall, TSLA stock ended Q1 2025 as its worst quarter since 2022 with a loss of 35%.

Breaking Down Tesla's Q1 Numbers with MSD Data

In Q1 2025, Tesla delivered 336,681 vehicles, falling short of analysts’ expectations of 377,592, based on a consensus of 27 estimates compiled by the company.

According to Main Street Data, Tesla delivered 324,000 units of the Model 3 and Model Y, while 12,900 units came from other models, including the Cybertruck. The Model Y continued to perform well, remaining one of Tesla's top-selling vehicles.

Tesla Energy Surges with Strong YoY Growth

Main Street Data further highlights Tesla’s Energy division’s strong performance in Q1. The company deployed 10.4k MWh (megawatt-hour) of energy storage products in Q1 compared to 4.05k MWh a year ago. This marked the company's second-highest quarter (after Q4 2024) for energy deployments.

Notably, Tesla’s energy storage deployed refers to the total capacity of its battery storage systems installed within a given period. This includes products such as Powerwall, Powerpack, and Megapack for homes and businesses.

Tesla's Energy division often flies under the radar despite its rapid expansion worldwide. With large-scale energy storage projects being deployed globally, the company recorded 31.5K MWh in deployments last year. If the current growth trajectory holds, 2025 could set a new benchmark for Tesla's energy business. Although Tesla’s Energy division delivered strong results, investors are largely focused and concerned about the company's automotive segment.

What Is the Prediction for Tesla Stock?

On Wall Street, analysts have maintained a neutral stance on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 14 Buys, 11 Holds, and 12 Sells assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Tesla shares is $315.0, suggesting a potential upside of 11.4% from the current level.

See more TSLA analyst ratings

data.title: Defense Stocks Surge on Reports of Musk's Reduced Government Role
data.description:
Defense contractor stocks surged on Wednesday after Politico reported that Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk is expected to step back from his role as a special advisor to President Trump. Investors viewed this news as a potential indicator of reduced pressure on defense spending cuts.

Musk’s tenure in the Trump administration included leading the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), an agency focused on streamlining government operations and undertaking defense budget reductions.

This development is seen as positive for companies heavily reliant on government contracts and defense-related revenue. These include Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH), Palantir (PLTR), Lockheed Martin (LMT), and L3Harris Technologies (LHX), which rose by 5.1%, 3.3%, 1%, and 1.03%, respectively.

At the same time, aerospace contractors, Rocket Lab (RKLB), Intuitive Machines (LUNR), and AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), gained 6.6%, 11.2%, and 2.6%, respectively, due to the implication of a change in government spending priorities.

Is Aerospace and Defense a Good Investment?

The aerospace and defense market are projected to grow from $888.94 billion in 2024 to $1,404.12 billion by 2033, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.88%.

The space industry is growing fast, with more demand for satellites and space technologies, thereby aiding the growth of aerospace companies. Simultaneously, defense companies are benefiting as countries increase spending on military equipment and services due to rising global tensions. Defense spending hit $2.4 trillion in 2023 and is expected to keep growing.

With this background, let's take a look at which aerospace and defense stock is a Buy. According to the TipRanks Comparison tool, both ASTS and LHX stocks have a Strong Buy consensus rating. In terms of upside, analysts see the most upside in LUNR stock of 92.7%, followed by ASTS stock of 71.7%.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: AMZN, COST, WMT, TGT, and NKE: Retail Stocks Plunge as 'Liberation Day' Tariff Sparks Fears of Soaring Prices
data.description:
Retail stocks took a big hit after President Donald Trump rolled out sweeping tariffs, sending shockwaves through the market. Shares of Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), and Nike (NKE) tumbled as investors braced for rising costs that could squeeze profits and push up prices. Even major players like Amazon (AMZN) and Costco (COST) saw their stocks slide more than 5%.

The new tariff plan slaps a 10% duty on all imports starting April 5, with an even steeper rate for 60 countries, including China (34%), Vietnam (46%), and the EU (20%). These rates stack on top of existing tariffs, making imports much pricier.

Analysts and Investors Sound Alarm on Tariff Shock

With the full scope of tariffs confirmed, industry experts warn that retailers have few ways to escape rising costs. GlobalData retail managing director Neil Saunders called the new tariff regime "shocking" in both its breadth and high rates, emphasizing that "there is no escape from tariffs." Every importing company must now grapple with these higher costs.

Jonathan Gold, vice president of supply chain and customs policy at the National Retail Federation, pointed out that the uncertainty leading up to the announcement left retailers with little time to "really plan and prepare."

Meanwhile, shifting production away from China, Mexico, and Canada, once a go-to strategy for avoiding tariffs, is no longer enough. Gold explained that with higher tariffs now hitting a broader range of key exporter countries, retailers have even fewer options to cushion the impact.

According to CFRA analyst Arun Sundaram, the near-term consequences will be felt on margins, as "tariffs are realized first before companies start to mitigate" the added costs.

Can Subscription Giants Like Amazon and Costco Dodge the Pain?

Retailers with membership-based business models, such as Amazon and Costco, may be in a stronger position to navigate these challenges. Unlike traditional retailers, they can use membership fees to offset some of the increased costs, allowing them to keep prices more stable for customers.

Costco is already using its size and supplier relationships to negotiate better deals. Meanwhile, Amazon, despite its vast revenue streams, including its booming cloud business, faces challenges. CEO Andy Jassy warned that third-party sellers, who rely heavily on imports, could struggle to keep prices low.

Still, even these giants won't escape unscathed. While they may delay price hikes longer than competitors, rising costs will eventually reach consumers.

Which Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the five stocks mentioned above, analysts think that Amazon stock has the most room to run. In fact, AMZN's average price target of $269.34 per share implies more than 37% upside potential. At the same time, analysts expect the least from COST stock, as its average price target of $1,088.25 equates to a gain of 12.76%.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: AAPL, NVDA, AMZN: Tech Stocks Plunge as Trump Announces 34% Tariff on Chinese Imports
data.description:
The stocks of leading technology companies such as Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), and Amazon (AMZN) are each down 5% after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a 34% tariff on Chinese goods.

Chinese imports were among the hardest hit as President Trump announced that a baseline tariff of 10% would be imposed on imports from most countries around the world. The European Union was slapped with a 20% across the board tariff, while India had a 26% duty levied on its products sent to the U.S.

However, it was news of the 34% tariff on Chinese products that prompted an immediate reaction in technology stocks. Apple, which manufactures its iPhones and other consumer electronics in China, saw its stock fall 7% in after hours trading as Trump announced the new trade levies.

The World’s Factory Floor

China, whose cheap labor and manufacturing capabilities have earned it a reputation as the “world’s factory floor,” is where many technology companies either manufacture electronics outright or source many of their needed components. As such, the tariffs announced on China have caused chaos and uncertainty for many tech companies.

Other technology companies with a presence in China whose share price is down 5% or more on Trump’s tariff announcement include Tesla (TSLA), Meta Platforms (META), and chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO). It’s not clear which, if any, technology companies might be given exemptions from Trump’s tariffs, most of which go into effect immediately.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 32 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy, 11 Hold, and four Sell recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $249.88 implies 11.61% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on AAPL stock

data.title: Is Conagra Brands (CAG) a Good Stock to Buy before Earnings?
data.description:
Shares of packaged foods company Conagra Brands (CAG) fell in today's trading as investors await its Q3 earnings results on April 3 before the market opens. Analysts are expecting earnings per share to come in at $0.52 on revenue of $2.9 billion. This represents a decline from the $0.69 per share seen in the year-ago period, according to TipRanks' data.

However, it seems likely that the firm will likely beat earnings estimates, as it has done so in all but two quarters since its 2021 Q4. As a result, it is still possible that Conagra will be able to exceed last year’s figure. Interestingly, though, Morgan Stanley does not seem too enthusiastic about the stock.

Indeed, the investment firm recently started covering Conagra Brands with a Hold rating and a $27 price target, which basically implies that shares are already close to fair value. This is because the bank believes that sales growth for the overall industry is slow and prices are hard to raise, which is a problem since costs are going up. This has led to the firm’s return on assets and equity to trend lower over the past few years, according to Main Street Data.

What Do Options Traders Anticipate?

Using TipRanks’ Options tool, we can see what options traders are expecting from the stock immediately after its earnings report. Indeed, the at-the-money straddle suggests that options traders expect a 4.7% price move in either direction. This estimate is derived from the $26.50 strike price, with call options priced at $0.55 and put options at $0.70.

Is CAG Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on CAG stock based on one Buy, 11 Hold, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CAG price target of $27.18 per share implies 2.8% upside potential.

See more CAG analyst ratings
